perturbation.original_text: Area of Non-Competition means the State of Zacatecas, Mexico and the area extending 20 kilometers in all directions from the external boundary of each mineral property owned or controlled by Western Silver or in which Western Silver holds any legal interest, in Mexico, as at the Effective Date
perturbation.changed_text: Area of Non-Competition means the State of Zacatecas, Mexico
perturbation.explanation: Removing the definition of the 'Area of Non-Competition' beyond the State of Zacatecas, Mexico introduces ambiguity. The subsequent references to the Area of Non-Competition become unclear as the exact geographical limits are not consistently defined. This creates an in-text contradiction with section 2.1, which refers to mining activities in a geographical area beyond Zacatecas itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Western Copper covenants and agrees with Glamis that, for a period of 2 years after the Effective Date, it will not, directly or indirectly, either individually or in partnership or jointly or in conjunction with any Person, which will include being a principal, agent, shareholder, or advisor of such Person or in any other manner whatsoever, (a) carry on or be engaged in Mining Activities, or i 162967.3 -4- (b) advise, lend money to, guarantee the debts or obligations of or permit its name to be used by any Person who carries on or is engaged in Mining Activities, in the Area of Non-Competition.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines the 'Area of Non-Competition' to include the State of Zacatecas and a 20km radius around Western Silver's mineral properties. The changed text removes the 20km radius, effectively shrinking the defined area. The contradicted text (Section 2.1) prohibits mining activities within the 'Area of Non-Competition'. Because the definition of the 'Area of Non-Competition' has been narrowed by the changed text, section 2.1's prohibition now applies to a smaller geographical area. This creates a direct contradiction, because activities that were prohibited previously are now permitted because they may fall outside the new, smaller 'Area of Non-Competition'. This constitutes an 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' as essential language has been removed, creating a contradiction with constraints stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: Designated Mineral Property means a mineral property or individual mineral concession within a mineral property, that is owned or controlled by Western Silver or in which Western Silver holds any legal interest in Mexico as of the Effective Date
perturbation.changed_text: Designated Mineral Property means a mineral property or individual mineral concession.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the phrase 'that is owned or controlled by Western Silver or in which Western Silver holds any legal interest in Mexico as of the Effective Date' from the definition of 'Designated Mineral Property,' the scope of what constitutes a 'Designated Mineral Property' becomes ambiguous. This creates an in-text contradiction when Section 2.2 refers to the disposition of a Designated Mineral Property that Glamis acquired under the Arrangement since the definition no longer limits the property to what Western Silver owned or controlled.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Glamis covenants and agrees with Western Copper that if at any time it intends to dispose of a Designated Mineral Property for cash consideration or by abandonment, it will give Western Copper notice (the ' Disposition Notice") of the intended disposition. For a period of 30 days from the time of delivery of the Disposition Notice Glamis will, if requested by Western Silver, entertain an offer from Western Copper to acquire the Designated Mineral Property. In the ease of a Designated Mineral Property that Glamis intends to dispose of for cash consideration, the parties will negotiate in good faith to reach a mutually agreeable agreement for the sale to Western Copper of the [Designated Mineral Property. If Glamis and Western Copper are unable to negotiate an acceptable agreement with respect to the Designated Mineral Property within the 30 day period, Glamis may thereafter dispose of the Designated Mineral Property as it sees til in its absolute discretion. If Glamis does not dispose of the Designated Mineral Property within a period of 3 months from the first to occur of the date that Glamis and Western Copper acknowledge failure to negotiate an acceptable agreement with respect to the Designated Mineral Property and the end of the 30 day period, the provisions of this section 2.2 will once again apply to any intended disposition of the Designated Mineral Property by Glamis. In the case of a Designated Mineral Property that Glamis intends to abandon, Glamis wall, if requested by Western Copper, transfer such Designated Mineral Property to Western Copper at no cost save and except for the reasonable costs of transfer incurred by Glamis.
perturbation.justification: The omission of 'that is owned or controlled by Western Silver or in which Western Silver holds any legal interest in Mexico as of the Effective Date' broadens the definition of 'Designated Mineral Property.' This creates a contradiction because Section 2.2 explicitly refers to the disposition of a Designated Mineral Property that Glamis acquired under the Arrangement. The revised definition no longer confines the property to what Western Silver owned, making the disposition obligation ambiguous and potentially encompassing properties outside the original scope. This makes the text in Section 2.2 related to Glamis disposing of 'Designated Mineral Property' acquired under the arrangement contradictory because 'Designated Mineral Property' is no longer limited to property that was owned/controlled by Western Silver.

perturbation.original_text: Any remedy expressly set forth in this §3.1 wall be in addition to and not inclusive of or dependent upon the exercise of any other remedy available to Glamis at law or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: Any remedy expressly set forth in this §3.1 wall be dependent upon the exercise of any other remedy available to Glamis at law or otherwise.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'not inclusive of or dependent upon' to 'dependent upon', the clause now states that the remedies are dependent on other legal remedies, directly contradicting the original intent of cumulative remedies. This introduces an in-text contradiction because it limits the independent application of the contract's specific remedies, making them contingent on external legal actions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No remedy provided for in this Agreement is intended to be exclusive of any other remedy and each such remedy will be cumulative and will be in addition to every other remedy given hereunder or available at law or in equity,
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that remedies in §3.1 are 'dependent upon' other legal remedies, while the contradicted text explicitly states that remedies are 'not intended to be exclusive of any other remedy' and are 'cumulative'. This creates a direct contradiction, as the changed text makes the contract's remedies contingent on external legal actions, while the original text ensures the contract's remedies are independently applicable and additive to other available remedies.
